title: Data Dictionary for allegheny_dog_licenses.csv
description:
**Description**

This dog‑license dataset contains 50 013 distinct records, each identified by a unique integer key.  
The majority of licenses are lifetime and spayed or neutered, with the most common type being *Dog Lifetime Spayed Female* (≈33 % of records). The breed field is highly heterogeneous, but “Other” accounts for more than half the entries, indicating many unclassified or mixed breeds.  
Coat color is similarly diverse; “Other” colors comprise a third of the records, while common single‑tone or simple pattern colors such as black, brown, and white dominate the remainder.  
Dog names are largely unique – over 90 % of names fall into an “Other” bucket that aggregates all uncommon names, with a handful of popular choices like *Bella*, *Lucy*, and *Daisy*.  
All licenses expire in the year 2099, while their effective dates span from early 2003 to mid‑2026, revealing a long‑term dataset with an uneven temporal distribution.  
Owner ZIP codes cluster in the 150 00–479 09 range, but a majority of records are labeled “Other”, reflecting many unique or unrecorded ZIP codes.

**Notable Characteristics**

- **High “Other” prevalence** in *Breed*, *Color*, *DogName*, and *OwnerZip* (≥58 % in each), suggesting incomplete or unstandardized categorization.  
- **Sparse missing data**: only 50 records lack a coat color; the remaining fields are complete except for one missing breed value.  
- **Uniform expiration year** (2099) indicates a single‑year horizon and limits temporal analysis across years.  
- **Skewed ValidDate distribution**: a large concentration of dates in 2006, with long tails toward 2017–2025, implying a non‑uniform issuance pattern.  
- **No duplicate records**: the `_id` field is strictly unique and matches the record count.  
- **Potential PII/PHI**: dog names, ZIP codes, and license validity dates can be used for demographic or location profiling.  
- **Data quality concerns**: high “Other” counts and missing color values reduce the usefulness of categorical analyses without further data cleaning or enrichment.  

---

_id.title: Unique Key
_id.description: A system-generated positive integer that uniquely identifies each dog license record.

LicenseType.title: License Type
LicenseType.description: The status of the dog license, indicating whether it is a lifetime, senior, spayed/neutered, or other special case. Used to determine regulatory requirements and fee schedules.

Breed.title: Dog Breed
Breed.description: The registered or mixed‑breed category of the dog, such as Labrador Retriever or Mixed. This field is part of the dog's profile used for breed‑specific health monitoring.

x-qsv.example_counts:

Color.title: Coat Color
Color.description: The predominant color or coat pattern of the dog’s fur, including single colors and multi‑tone patterns. This attribute is useful for identification and breed‑specific health studies.

DogName.title: Dog Name
DogName.description: The chosen name of the dog, which may be a common canine nickname or a unique personal name. It is part of the dog’s identity but does not appear in any formal registry.

OwnerZip.title: Owner ZIP Code
OwnerZip.description: The five‑digit postal code of the dog owner’s residential location. This can be joined with demographic or geographic datasets for regional analysis.

ExpYear.title: License Expiration Year
ExpYear.description: The year in which the dog license expires. All records currently show 2099, indicating a uniform expiration horizon for this dataset.

ValidDate.title: License Valid From
ValidDate.description: The date and time at which the dog license becomes effective, recorded in ISO‑8601 UTC format. Together with ExpYear it defines the active license period for each dog.
